summary: Add support for custom font colors in the TabBar

description.text: Partially Addresses: https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/9161 Related: #88709, #106164 The inspiration for this PR was #106164 and the desire to set a custom color for the `Output` and `Debugger` tabs now that they are within a TabBar, but there is a desire to do such, as evidenced by the previous proposal. Currently it is impossible to set colors individually for tabs in a TabBar. This PR adds support to set custom overrides for the selected, unselected, hovered, and disabled colors on a *per tab* basis.

summary: Fix Context Menu Disappearing

description.text:
Referring to this issue #105838 

It appears the last else if statement of EditorHelpBitTooltip::_notification is unnecessarily using mouse velocity to dismiss the tooltip box, which is different from the default tooltip(for non-XML context), and causes the tooltip box for XML context to disappear with gradual movements.

This else if section tries to mitigate multiple tooltip boxes from popping up when the cursor moves across multiple elements at high speed. This behavior is already accomplished by the preceding case statements thus removing the else if statement fixes the issure while maintaining the desired behavior.

* *Bugsquad edit, fixes: #105838*

summary: Refine Euler Rotation dropdown menu and tooltip descriptions

description.text:

Modified tags for dropdown menu for Node3D-Transform-Rotation Order in node_3d.cpp to use lower-case xyz to indicate intrinsic rotation, since capitalized XYZ typically indicates extrinsic rotation.

Modified tooltip content when hovering cursor on the Rotation Order field to indicate Godot uses intrinsic rotation implicitly.

summary: Do not grab focus on filename `LineEdit` in `EditorFileDialog` when outside the tree

description.text:
<!--
Please target the `master` branch in priority.

Relevant fixes are cherry-picked for stable branches as needed by maintainers.

To speed up the contribution process and avoid CI errors, please set up pre-commit hooks locally:
https://docs.godotengine.org/en/latest/contributing/development/code_style_guidelines.html
-->

When a POT is generated for the first time, `last_pot_path` is added to the project metadata and this value gets used the next time the Editor runs to configure the POT generator dialog.

The problem is that currently the code configures the dialog too early and an error shows up the next time the Editor runs:

```
ERROR: Condition "!is_inside_tree()" is true.
   at: grab_focus (scene/gui/control.cpp:2210)
```

This PR fixes this issue.

To reproduce the bug, simply run the following project in the CLI:

[POTGeneratorDialogBug.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20144111/POTGeneratorDialogBug.zip)

summary: Refactor AssetLib pagination

description.text:
Part of #104574.

This PR refactored pagination related UI elements into a `EditorAssetLibraryPagination` class.

- The main reason is that button texts like First, Prev, Next, and Last need context when translating, so they have to be handled manually in `NOTIFICATION_TRANSLATION_CHANGED`. However, the AssetLib screen has two sets of pagination controls (top & bottom) and they are recreated when the search result is updated. It's too cumbersome to track them separately with 8 variables.
- Page buttons originally added space characters around the page number to prevent them from being too narrow. This PR changes that to setting a minimum rect width.
- Cleaned up header includes.

summary: Add a deferred rendering mode to the Forward+ renderer

description.text:
This is a proof of concept implementation for a deferred clustered renderer as an option in the Forward+ renderer of Godot.
You can enable deferred rendering in the project settings (Driver section, below depth prepass setting).

It changes the existing scene shader and skips the directional and positional lighting stages instead writing out data to a set of g-buffers (currently albedo, position, normal and ORM data).
We also write out ambient and GI data to our color buffer, there may be a case for moving this in a separate pass as well but it seems overkill at this point.

The lighting code has now moved to a post process. This is a compute shader that reads the data from the g-buffers and sets things up as if we're continuing the fragment shader of the scene shader. Then applies the same lighting code.

The transparent pass uses the normal forward approach, deferred is only applied for opaque rendering.

The current solution works with depth pre-pass however it may be more performant to disable this.

Still left to do before this is functional:
- [ ] fix issue that prevents this from working in the editor (weird sampler not recognised as a sampler bug, IDK) 
- [ ] optimise compiled variants based on whether deferred rendering is enabled
- [ ] provide access to the cluster data
- [ ] figure out if we can store layer data or our instance index, currently all lights effect all geometry (except unshaded)
- [ ] apply omni and spotlight lighting code

The solution does support multiview however there is a pre-existing issue with clustering for which the workaround does not work with deferred rendering. We may need to change the code that builds the clustering to create a cluster buffer per eye.

There are many features that apply lighting on a per instance basis or for other reasons can not work with deferred rendering. An incomplete list is below:
- Godot shader user lighting shader code will be ignore, some output of the user fragment shader code will have no effect, we'll need to put together a full list of these
- MSAA can't be used with deferred rendering (at this time, possibly never)
- SSAO/SSR/SSIL etc are likely broken, these may be fixable with a little extra work
- Fog is currently broken, we may be able to introduce this
- `shadow_to_opacity` (unless combined with unshaded) won't work.
- all geometry (except unshaded) receive shadows
- transmittence, rim, clearcoat and similar settings will be ignored

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3918b979-3f40-43f7-8125-e81ec73a84d2)

summary: Godot 4.4.1 Using Wrong System Theming on KDE-based Systems

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in 4.4.1 stable (official app image) and 4.4.1-fedora (rpm). Not reproducible in flatpak due to sandboxing; I know the cause of this bug.

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Fedora Linux 42 (KDE Plasma Desktop Edition) on Wayland - Wayland display driver, Single-window, 3 monitors - Vulkan (Mobile) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 7800 XT (RADV NAVI32) - AMD Ryzen 7 5800X3D 8-Core Processor (16 threads)

### Issue description

Godot does not respect system theming for cursors properly on KDE Plasma (might also be the case on other DEs). Instead of pulling from the user's cursor theme, it seems to fallback to the `/usr/share/icons/default/index.theme`, which is the fallback cursor for most Linux distros (Adwaita). This means that the cursor, when mousing over godot, transforms into the Adwaita cursor rather than using my theme's cursor (Breeze). This does not happen in the flatpak due to the nature of sandboxing, and lack of access to system files from the flatpak.

This is improper behavior. All programs should be reading from user theming settings, and not falling all the way back to core system fallback settings. Usually, this problem happens on really old programs, which is how I knew where to look. Upon changing `/usr/share/icons/default/index.theme` to have `breeze_default` as its cursor as opposed to Adwaita, the problem went away, but users should not have to change system defaults in order to properly theme a user application.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/78b53b31-59b8-44d1-8ebc-f9cabf36dba5)
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8b8205b3-7e65-4c26-bb25-60ec3c7a927c)

Default value of `/usr/share/icons/default/index.theme`
```
[Icon Theme]
Inherits=Adwaita
```
My modified `/usr/share/icons/default/index.theme` (Even though this fixes the problem, I should not have done this. Do not do this. This is a bad idea. But it demonstrates what the problem is for the purpose of the bug report.)
```
[Icon Theme]
Inherits=breeze_cursors
```

### Steps to reproduce

1. Open Godot when your cursor theme is set to anything but Adwaita
2. Mouse over the godot editor

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

This is present on an editor level, meaning it happens without any project open whatsoever. N/A

summary: Godot (including the editor) freezes when certain Godots own functions are used in threads, that are waiting to finish.

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.4.1.stable

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable.mono - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4070 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7283) - 13th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-13700KF (24 threads)

### Issue description

Godot (including the editor) freezes when certain functions are used in a thread.

**Info:** Certain functions are treated differently than "normal" functions when running in a separate thread. These functions are identified in the code by special macros: FUNC[NN][C/R]. If these functions detect, that they are not running in the server-thread (by comparing Thread::get_caller_id() != server_thread), then they don't simply return their results, but are handled by special functions (e.g., push_ret() or push() of the CommandQueueMT class). These functions ultimately all result in locking a mutex and then waiting for it to be released (or, more precisely, for the "notify_all" function, which is regularly called from the main thread via the sync functions – for example, on the RenderingServer – in Main::iteration()).

The point is, if you wait for a thread to end (with thread.wait_to_finish()) in the main-thread ... then you get a deadlock, which freezes the game and often also the editor.

### Steps to reproduce

Lets take the simple example from [the docs](https://docs.godotengine.org/en/stable/tutorials/performance/using_multiple_threads.html) and squeezes just a little bit (add a delay to give it some time and then call a function).

All you have to do is to assign this script to a simple scene node, run this scene and click on the close button to let the magic happen. I prepared two different functions:

1. `RenderingServer.get_rendering_device()` - a "normal" function, directly executed. Whether you wait three seconds or click the Close button earlier, the scene will end cleanly.
2. `RenderingServer.get_video_adapter_vendor()` - a "capsulated" function ... here you will get the deadlock/freeze.

```gdscript
var thread: Thread

# The thread will start here.
func _ready():
	thread = Thread.new()
	# You can bind multiple arguments to a function Callable.
	thread.start(_thread_function.bind("Wafflecopter"))


# Run here and exit.
# The argument is the bound data passed from start().
func _thread_function(userdata):

	# === EXTRA LINES START ===
	OS.delay_msec(3000) # this is just to verify, that the following function is not called to fast
	RenderingServer.get_rendering_device() # the "normal" function
	# RenderingServer.get_video_adapter_vendor() # the "capsulated" function
	# === EXTRA LINES ENDS ===

	# Print the userdata ("Wafflecopter")
	print("I'm a thread! Userdata is: ", userdata)


# Thread must be disposed (or "joined"), for portability.
func _exit_tree():
	thread.wait_to_finish()
```

What makes it particularly controversial is the fact that many functions in the RenderingServer, the PhysicsServer2D and the PhysicsServer3D are affected.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

I don't think providing a MRP is necessary, but I can do it any time.

Best regards, Opadin.

summary: Control's `find_valid_focus_neighbor` not working

### System information

### Issue description

Using Control's `find_valid_focus_neighbor()` doesn't find the appropriate neighbor. 

I would expect the behavior to match the focus shifting displayed when using the default UI input actions.

Here's an example of code I expected to mirror the default behavior but doesn't:

```gdscript
    func _unhandled_input(event: InputEvent) -> void:
	var next_focus: Control
	
	if event.is_action_pressed("ui_ext_left"):
		print("left")
		next_focus = find_valid_focus_neighbor(SIDE_LEFT)
	elif event.is_action_pressed("ui_ext_right"):
		print("right")
		next_focus = find_valid_focus_neighbor(SIDE_RIGHT)
	elif event.is_action_pressed("ui_ext_up"):
		print("up")
		next_focus = find_valid_focus_neighbor(SIDE_TOP)
	elif event.is_action_pressed("ui_ext_down"):
		print("down")
		next_focus = find_valid_focus_neighbor(SIDE_BOTTOM)
	
	if next_focus:
		print(next_focus)
		next_focus.grab_focus()
```

In this example, the action will be printed to the console but `next_focus` continually fails to be set to a valid Control.

I have been able to get it to partially work in one project by manually setting the `focus_neighbor_*` on the Controls from the inspector but I haven't been able to reproduce it since.
And since I'm trying to dynamically add controls, this would still have been less than ideal.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Set up custom input mappings
2. Create a scene with a Control container
3. Add several buttons to it
4. Create a script which uses `find_valid_focus_neighbor()` and `grab_focus()` to shift focus using the custom input mappings
5. Run the scene
6. Select a button
7. Navigate using the arrow keys (this should shift focus as expected)
8. Navigate using the custom input mappings (this doesn't work)

summary: Script Editor: Fix Ctrl-Drag unique-name Editable Children

description.text:
Fixes #104545

- Don't use unique name when the node dragged can't be accessed by the current node via `%`

Note that `_find_script_node()` originally only search in nodes owned by the current scene. This dates back to the Godot is Open Source commit and should have been an early overthought. summary: Lighting on `MeshInstance3D` with non-uniform-scale flickers when rotated

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in 4.3.stable, 4.4.stable, 4.5.dev3.

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 11 (build 22631) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1660 SUPER (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6636) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-10700F CPU @ 2.90GHz (16 threads)

### Issue description

When a `MeshInstance3D` has a non-uniform scale which meets the following conditions, its lighting will flicker when it is rotated:
- Two of the components have the ratio of **exactly** `0.9` between them (e.g. `1.0` and `0.9`)
- The remaining component must have the same value as one of the other two.
Examples of scales which will exhibit this issue:
- `(1, 0.9, 1)`
- `(0.9, 1, 0.9)`
- `(2, 2, 1.8)`
- `(3.6, 4, 4)`
Examples of scales which will not exhibit this issue:
- `(1, 0.9, 0.7)`
- `(0.9, 0.9, 0.9)`

This happens in editor as well as runtime. It happens in all three renderers.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/82573a1a-6701-49e9-aa69-05f43abcc4f2)

EDIT:
- Someone noted that the background is flickering as well - that is just a red herring from the GIF compression.

### Steps to reproduce

Open the attached project/scene, rotate the `MeshInstance3D` slowly, and note the flickering.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[bug-report-2.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20088559/bug-report-2.zip)

summary: Add "Show Encryption Key" toggle

description.text:
**What**
I changed the behavior of the Encryption Key LineEdit

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/41e46ad7-1b1f-40be-b414-26ba02314951)

1. By default, secrets should be hidden, and only displayed if requested by user.

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/96843318-f08f-4bfd-9183-18549b5c74f2)

2. A toggle option to display the Encryption Key of the project export windows.

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9477bfdc-8a88-4a50-afc5-82001e8a9091)

3. The key becomes hidden again when Encrypt Export is deactivated (reactivating it does not reactivate the “show key” toggle).

**Why?**
- As a general rule, “secret” strings should not be readable by default.
- Avoid key leaking by mistake (e.g. visible on screenshots, displayed by mistake, etc.)

summary: Reimport breaks bone positions

description.text:
### Tested versions

Godot 4.4

### System information

Linux Mint

### Issue description

When reimporting fbx models with armatures, inherited scenes break. This seems to be tied to the animation player.
This happens even if the armature was not changed in Blender. My concrete example is a pistol with several bones. In the fbx import view the gun is shown correctly and when creating a new inherited scene it also shows it correctly. However when updating an existing scene, multiple bones of the gun object suddenly take on seemingly random rotations and positions. It should be noted that the skeleton itself is displayed correctly however the actual bone attachment nodes are displaced for some reason. The bones are even marked as having being changed from their original position, clicking reset will reset them, until you save at which point they will revert to their broken state as long as an active animation player is in the scene.

The animation player however does not even have tracks for all the parameters edited here, some bones aren't touched at all yet they are somehow altered by it.
This makes working exceedingly frustrating because every tiny change in blender will result in me having to either remake the entire scene or remake all animations.

### Steps to reproduce

-Create a blender scene with multiple objects and bones
-import as fbx
-create inhereted scene and add animation player with bone animations
-change something in the blender scene so it triggers a reimport
-the inhereted scene is now broken

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A

Godot is being crashed attempting to import any 3D model that includes a texture. The problem is in how textures are being imported. summary: Improve script backtrace print in crash handlers

description.text:
- Follow-up to #91006.

Also fix the editor crash handler message for bug reports not properly using the `.editor` override.

Before this change, `-- END OF SCRIPT BACKTRACE --` would always be printed, even if it's a pure C++ crash with no script backtrace. That's because `ScriptServer::capture_script_backtraces` is never empty (unless there's no script language compiled in) as it includes empty backtraces for languages with no backtrace.

Reproduced in Godot v4.4.0.stable.official

### System information

Windows 11 - Godot v4.4.0.stable.official - Vukan (Forward+) - Intel UHD iGPU

### Issue description

Under the Vulkan (Forward+) renderer with SDFGI enabled, any `StandardMaterial3D` with `metallic > 0` and `roughness < 1` renders as a hyper-bright white surface—almost as if it’s self-illuminated—completely ignoring both albedo and roughness. Disabling SDFGI, switching to OpenGL3/GLES3, or using a discrete GPU produces the correct PBR appearance.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a fresh Godot 4.4 project.  
2. Add a 3D scene with a `MeshInstance3D` using a `BoxMesh` and a `Camera3D`.  
3. Assign a `StandardMaterial3D` to the sphere:  
   - `Metallic = 1.0`  
   - `Roughness = 0.1`  
   - `Albedo Color` = mid-gray (or any non-white)  
4. Add a `WorldEnvironment` node, enable **SDFGI** (default settings).  
5. In **Project → Rendering → Quality**, set **Driver** to **Forward+ (Vulkan)**.  
6. Run on an Intel UHD GPU.  

**Result:**
The box appears as an extremely bright white object—roughness is ignored and albedo is super bright, giving it a glowing-white look rather than a metallic one.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

---

**Workarounds tested:**
- Disable SDFGI: Vulkan path renders correctly.  
- Switch to OpenGL3/GLES3: Correct under both SDFGI on/off.  
- Run on discrete NVIDIA GPU: Correct under Vulkan+SDFGI.  

---

**Additional notes:**
- When `Metallic = 0` (dielectric) or `Roughness = 1` (fully diffuse), rendering is normal even with SDFGI on.  
- No errors or warnings emitted.  
- Bug appears specific to Vulkan Forward+ with SDFGI on Intel iGPUs—likely a shader or sampling fallback issue.

---

summary: Cannot connect to signals of internal nodes

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in 4.4.1-stable
- Untested in 4.3.x
- Cannot reproduce in 4.2

### System information

Windows 11 - Vulkan 1.3.271 - Forward+ - Using Device #0: NVIDIA - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3060 Laptop GPU   --- Also confirmed on Linux and MacOS

### Issue description

We are working on [a software dor editing and simulating molecular systems](https://github.com/MSEP-one)

In the past i used to [connect to signals from the internal LineEdit of the FileDialog to add some extra functionality for autocompletion](https://github.com/MSEP-one/msep.one/blob/main/godot_project/editor/controls/general/nano_file_dialog.gd#L14)

But the signals stopped working when we upgraded the project to 4.4.1

### Steps to reproduce

I have a minimal reproduction project, but here is a simple script that should work as well:
```
extends FileDialog

func _ready() -> void:
	# 1. Show the dialog
	popup_centered(Vector2(400, 330))
	# 2. Connect to signals
	var line_edit: LineEdit = _get_file_dialog_line_edit()
	if not line_edit.text_changed.is_connected(_on_file_dialog_line_edit_text_changed):
		line_edit.gui_input.connect(_on_file_dialog_line_edit_gui_input)
		line_edit.text_changed.connect(_on_file_dialog_line_edit_text_changed)


func _get_file_dialog_line_edit() -> LineEdit:
	var body_container: VBoxContainer = get_child(3, true) as VBoxContainer
	assert(is_instance_valid(body_container), "Invalid child of FileDialog, Did engine version change from 4.1.1?")
	var footer_container: HBoxContainer = body_container.get_child(3, true) as HBoxContainer
	assert(is_instance_valid(footer_container), "Invalid child of body_container, Did engine version change from 4.1.1?")
	var line_edit: LineEdit = footer_container.get_child(1, true) as LineEdit
	assert(is_instance_valid(line_edit), "Invalid child of footer_container, Did engine version change from 4.1.1?")
	return line_edit

# function are no longer eing called since project upgrade
func _on_file_dialog_line_edit_gui_input(in_event: InputEvent) -> void:
	print("INPUT EVENT: ", in_event)

# function are no longer eing called since project upgrade
func _on_file_dialog_line_edit_text_changed(new_text: String) -> void:
	print("NEW TEXT: ", new_text)
```

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[internal-nodes-signals-test.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20068975/internal-nodes-signals-test.zip)

summary: EditorInterface.popup_create_dialog has hard-coded 'Change' button text

description.text:
### Tested versions

v4.4.stable.official [4c311cbee]

### System information

Godot v4.4.stable - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4060 Laptop GPU (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.5597) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i9-14900HX (32 threads)

### Issue description

I am aware the function is experimental, but I would still expect the text of the button to be 'Confirm' or 'Select' or 'OK', rather than 'Change'.

I'm guessing this code-path is hitting the same code as 'right-click -> change type', which is where this text is coming from. I would personally think it's OK to change 'Change' to 'OK' in *all* cases, to avoid adding complexity. 

Otherwise it would of course be possible to add a new optional argument into the function which allows selecting what text will appear on the button (just like the title is currently customizable). 

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ae5f2356-e119-4a9c-b684-c0cc41dbfa3d)

### Steps to reproduce

See MRP

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

My addon Create Actor is a full Godot project which you can use as a reproduction. Right-click on a file/folder and select 'Create Actor...'. This spawns this dialogue. 

https://github.com/SirLich/godot-create-actor
